notes: Auto-executed tool call: the agent invokes a tool mid-turn (reads a file), the tool auto-resolves with no user approval, and the turn completes — the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. full-lifecycle-toolcall maps to coverage_id auto-executed-tool-call; the top-level scenarios.json verify.has_waiting_state is claudecode-shaped (claudecode can gate via a PostToolUse permission hook), but pi has no such hook (toolCall/toolResult parse straight through, no gating), so the pi arc ends in ready, not waiting — the spec asserts the no-waiting invariant instead. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh, a tmux REPL), NOT headless `pi --print`. WHY interactive (drift fix): this cell was originally recorded HEADLESS and only validated 2/5 because `pi --print` exits the instant it emits the final reply, so the daemon misses the terminal working→ready settle before process_exited (concern C6). Keeping pi alive in the REPL across one send + wait_turn lets the daemon observe the full ready→working→ready settle cleanly, and the single tmux teardown then yields one clean process_exited — same proven shape as the sibling auto-executed-tool-call cell (5/5). Setup writes a fixture file into the cwd; the prompt asks pi to read that file and report its first line, forcing exactly one auto-executed read tool call inside the working span. C7 / session-identity: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/session-start and pi/auto-executed-tool-call). The TURN arc, by contrast, must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown then all pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. The tool call itself is observable in transcript.jsonl as pi toolCall / toolResult message events (parser.go), satisfying the scenario's contains_tool_call signal; the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the tool call is asserted only via the working span it sustains, not a dedicated phase. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); pi may emit more than one working→ready arc if a prompt produces multiple transcript-activity bursts — the spec asserts the first arc resets cleanly and never enters waiting, not an exact arc count. Stable structurally: a ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id with a tool round-trip in the working span, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Prompt requiring tool use → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
Tool auto-resolves and the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
